data.title: Duality Bio Shares Surge 91% in Hong Kong Debut, Defying Market Turmoil
data.description:
Duality Biotherapeutics made a stellar trading debut in Hong Kong on Tuesday morning as its shares soared over 91% at the opening bell, defying the stock market volatility caused by the tariff war between the world's two largest economies.

The Chinese biotech firm opened at HK$181 per share, well above its initial public offering price of HK$94.60. As of 10:41 a.m., Hong Kong time, the stock traded at HK$209.60.

The company's debut takes place against a backdrop of heightened uncertainty in global equity markets, driven by escalating US-China trade tensions.

data.title: Australia's 'Big Four' Banks Expected to Weather Weaker Economic Conditions Over 2025, Fitch Ratings Says
data.description:
Fitch Ratings expects the "Big Four" Australian banks to withstand an expected low-growth and rising unemployment environment over the current year due to significant buffers, the credit ratings agency said in a statement Tuesday.

The strong business profiles and solid financial metrics of National Australia Bank , ANZ Group Holdings , Westpac Banking , and Commonwealth Bank of Australia underpin their long-term issuer default rating of "AA-."

The outlook on Commonwealth Bank of Australia's rating was revised to positive, while the other three banks retain their stable outlooks. The revision reflects the outperformance of the lender's earning metrics. It also revised the funding and liquidity score of the four banks to "a+" from "a."

Collateral positions are strong across the banks on the back of strengthened mortgage underwriting. The lenders' asset quality is expected to weaken modestly, but impaired loans are forecast to peak this year. Their strong liquidity management continues to offset some of the risks from their reliance on offshore wholesale funding sources.

The Australian government is likely to support these banks in times of financial strain due to their systemic importance, Fitch said.

data.title: Valneva's Chikungunya Vaccine Gets Brazil Approval
data.description:
Valneva received market authorization from the Brazilian Health Regulatory Agency for its chikungunya vaccine, IXCHIQ.

The French specialty vaccine company said Monday that the approval covers the single-use vaccine for people aged at least 18 years in the endemic country. The approval enables the start of large-scale clinical trials in Brazil, including phase 4 trials supporting the vaccine's approval in the US and the European Union.

IXCHIQ is already approved for specific age groups in the EU, US, Canada, and the UK, with Valneva having applications pending to extend its use to adolescents in the US, Canada, and the UK.

data.title: Inner Mongolia ERDOS Resources 2024 Profit Falls 36%, Revenue Slips 7%
data.description:
Inner Mongolia ERDOS Resources posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 1.85 billion yuan, down 36% from 2.90 billion yuan the previous year.

Earnings per share slid to 0.66 yuan from 1.04 yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The cashmere apparel and metal producer's revenue declined 7.0% year over year to 28.4 billion yuan from 30.6 billion yuan.

Inner Mongolia ERDOS Resources declared a dividend of 0.60 per share.

Shares of the company were down 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Wuhan Dameng Database's 2024 Profit Jumps 22%; Shares Slide 7%
data.description:
Wuhan Dameng Database's attributable profit rose 22% to 361.9 million yuan in 2024 from 296.1 million yuan in 2023, according to an annual report filed Tuesday with the Shanghai bourse.

Earnings per share at the database management system design and development company increased 4.8% to 5.44 yuan from 5.19 yuan in the previous year.

Operating income grew 31% to 1.04 billion yuan from 794.3 million yuan a year earlier.

Dameng Database's shares slid nearly 7% during Tuesday's late-morning trading.

data.title: Zhejiang Longsheng Group Logs 32% Rise in 2024 Profit; Shares Up 6%
data.description:
Zhejiang Longsheng Group posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 2.03 billion yuan, up 32% from 1.53 billion yuan the previous year.

Earnings per share rose to 0.6320 yuan from 0.4861 yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The chemical manufacturer's revenue rose 3.8% year over year to 15.9 billion yuan from 15.3 billion yuan.

Zhejiang Longsheng Group declared a dividend of 0.25 per share.

Shares of the company were up 6% in recent trade.

data.title: South Korea, Vietnam Pledge Deeper Trade Ties and Energy Cooperation
data.description:
South Korea and Vietnam agreed to boost bilateral trade to $150 billion by 2030 and expand collaboration in nuclear power, liquefied natural gas, clean energy, and textiles during ministerial meetings in Hanoi.

The two sides also discussed free trade agreement amendments to ease customs, expand agri-trade, and enhance certification processes, the South Korean Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy said in a Tuesday release.

data.title: Italian Court Validates Class Action Against Stellantis Over Japanese-made Faulty Airbags
data.description:
An Italian civil court verified the validity of the class action against Stellantis (STLAM.MI, ) over Japanese-made faulty airbags in its vehicles, Reuters reported Monday, citing the European carmaker and two consumer groups behind the legal action.

Separate statements from consumer groups Codacons and Altroconsumo said the court hearing was adjourned to Nov. 21 after the court in Turin, Italy, ruled that the case was admissible. They said affected customers have 150 days to join the class action.

The European carmaker sourced the airbags under scrutiny in the case from Japanese automotive parts manufacturer Takata. In addition to the pending case in Italy, it is also facing a similar class action in France.

Stellantis told Reuters that the latest ruling only addressed the admissibility of the case, not its alleged liability or the damages claimed. It noted that it was working "tirelessly" on recalling affected models and reserving "the right to consider any appropriate initiative in the context of the ongoing proceedings."

Stellantis did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

data.title: Duality Biotherapeutics Raises HK$1.5 Billion From BioNTech-backed Hong Kong IPO
data.description:
Duality Biotherapeutics raised HK$1.51 billion in net proceeds from its IPO after pricing the shares at the lower end of the indicative price range.

The developer of antibody drug conjugates issued 17,332,300 shares at HK$94.60, the bottom end of its guidance of as high as HK$103.20 each, according to a late Monday disclosure.

The deal's Hong Kong public offering portion was 115.14 times subscribed, prompting the company to issue 7.5 million shares, a fivefold increase from the 1.5 million shares initially allotted.

The international offering portion was 13.52 times subscribed, and the firm issued 9.79 million shares, lower than the initial allocation of 13.56 million.

Germany's BioNTech and 10 private equity funds were cornerstone investors in the listing. They subscribed to a total of 5.34 million IPO shares.

Duality Bio starts trading on the Hong Kong bourse today, April 15.

data.title: Australia's Clarity Pharmaceutical gains on reporting first prostate cancer treatment
data.description:
** Shares of Clarity Pharmaceuticals rise 6% to A$1.850,

** Stock hits its highest level since April 3

** Radiopharmaceutical co announces the treatment of the first participant with the first dose of Cu-SAR-bisPSMA, a radioactive substance used to target prostate cancer cells

** CU6 down 55.7% YTD, including current session's moves

data.title: This iron ore miner is no Fortescue – But it plans to triple production in 2025
data.description:
Key points:
  • Fenix Resources plans to nearly triple iron ore production from 1.5Mtpa to 4Mtpa in 2024-25, with Shine mine at full capacity and Beebyn-W11 nearing production
  • Record shipments of 704,000 tonnes in March 2025 quarter, almost double last year’s, signal strong operational momentum
  • Bell Potter is Buy rated with a 41-cent target price (as at Jan-25) reflects confidence in Fenix’s low-capital growth and integrated logistics driving robust cash flows
  • Dividend pause in August 2024 to fund expansion caused an 11.9% share price drop, but analysts expect dividends to resume in 2025 with 5-6% yields by 2026-27

While iron ore remains a cornerstone of Australia’s export economy, the pool of pure-play investment opportunities is shrinking. Heavyweights like BHP and Rio Tinto are steadily shifting their focus to future-facing metals like copper and lithium. Among larger-cap iron ore companies, Fortescue — gradually diversifying into green energy — and Champion Iron stand out.

In terms of larger cap pure plays, this leaves us with Fortescue – which will eventually become a more diversified green energy producer – and Champion Iron.

However, at the smaller end of the market, Fenix Resources is arguably one of the only iron ore names poised for major growth this year.

Fenix at a Glance

Based in Western Australia’s Mid-West region, Fenix operates across three integrated divisions:

  • Iron Ore: Mining and exploration through its fully owned Iron Ridge mine (high-grade, direct shipping ore), Shine mine (open-pit, DSO), and W11 deposit (rights to mine up to 10Mt).

  • Logistics: Road and rail haulage solutions for Fenix’s operations and third-party clients.

  • Port Services: In-loading via truck or rail with secure storage for up to 400,000 tonnes at Fenix’s on-wharf facilities.

In FY24, Fenix generated $259.2 million in revenue, with $240.1 million driven by iron ore sales.

Scaling Up: From 1.5Mtpa to 4Mtpa

The 2024-25 period marks a pivotal shift for Fenix as it boosts iron ore production from 1.5 million tonnes to 4 million tonnes per annum. The company’s latest quarterly report (15 April 2025) highlighted recent developments: "Shine is now in full production, Iron Ridge continues to perform, and our third mine, Beebyn-W11, is moving rapidly towards first production."

The March 2025 quarter saw record shipments of 704,000 tonnes — nearly double the previous year’s figure — fueled by Shine’s ramp-up. Fenix also trimmed cash costs at Iron Ridge by 4.8%, dropping to A$73.8 per tonne from A$77.6 a year earlier, signaling operational efficiency.

Analyst Outlook

Bell Potter analysts reaffirmed a Buy rating in January 2025, with a 41-cent target price. Their optimism rests on Fenix’s ability to “expand its portfolio of low-capital mining assets and leverage integrated logistics to drive strong cash flows.” These cash flows are expected to fund growth initiatives and shareholder returns. Analysts project a significant earnings increase from FY24 to FY26, alongside a return to dividends:

2024a

Source: Bell Potter | January 2025

Recent performance

Fenix shares have remained flat year-to-date and over the past 12 months. A notable setback came in August 2024 when the company announced it would pause dividends to fund current growth plans.

The market did not take the news well, with Fenix shares down 11.9% on the day of the announcement (29-Aug-24). Compounding the pressure, iron ore prices slipped from ~US$100 to US$92 per tonne that month.

Historically, Fenix has been a reliable high-yield stock, delivering 10-15% annual dividend yields. The shift toward growth may have pushed out some income-focused investors, but the company’s progress on its expansion plans — and the anticipated resumption of dividends — could serve as a key catalyst for share price recovery.

data.title: Bank of Beijing's 2024 Profit Inches Up
data.description:
Bank of Beijing's attributable profit inched up 0.8% to 25.8 billion yuan in 2024 from 25.6 billion yuan in 2023, according to an annual report filed Tuesday with the Shanghai bourse.

The lender's earnings per share increased 0.9% to 1.07 yuan from 1.06 yuan in the previous year.

Operating income grew 4.8% to 69.9 billion yuan from 66.7 billion yuan a year earlier.

data.title: The bond market holds calmer for the time being
data.description:
US Treasury 30-year yields (%) daily chart

This will at least be one spot to feed into a calmer mood in broader markets for now. Long-end Treasuries are seeing some bids again, with yields backing away from the highs seen last week. 30-year yields are now down to 4.77%, falling a little further away from the 5% mark. Meanwhile, 10-year yields are seen at 4.34% today - down from a high of 4.59% on Friday last week.

As things stand, there's still a lot to digest with Trump's tariffs policy.

While we've gone through the initial reaction, it's now over to analysing the impact of it all. At the same time, market players will also have to deal with the constant changes in the level of tariffs while also accounting for any possible retaliation and escalation.

Taking that into consideration, it's hard to be too confident of a return to normality any time soon. That especially when US and China are still not seen making too much progress in striking an accord.

Xi is busy with his Southeast Asia tour this week and Trump is also not wanting to make the first move yet. So, there will definitely be economic pain to deal with during the interim.

As for Treasuries, it's not just the relative uncertainty of the tariffs policy. The impact on the US deficit, inflation, economy, and Fed reaction function all also needs to be factored into the equation. And that's the tough part at the moment, with investors already struggling for confidence amid the policy incoherence to begin with.

data.title: Small countries sit at nexus of tariff trade-offs
data.description:
The US put a 10% levy on Singapore and Australia despite having a goods surplus and strong military ties with them. Negotiating on the duty looks hard. But diversifying exports away from both states' top market, China, which Trump wants to squeeze, makes more sense than ever.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @AntonyMCurrie on X

CONTEXT NEWS

The Australian government on April 10 turned down a proposal from Beijing to "join hands" to counter U.S. tariffs. The offer came in an article China's ambassador to the country, Xiao Qian, wrote in The Age, a Melbourne-based newspaper.

Singapore's Prime Minister on April 8 said the trade levies imposed by the U.S. President Donald Trump "are not actions one does to a friend", echoing similar words spoken by Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese.

The Trump administration on April 2 said it would impose a 10% tariff on goods from Singapore and Australia, even though it has a trade surplus with both countries.

data.title: Sky Metals Begins Drill Program at New South Wales Tin Project; Shares Fall 7%
data.description:
Sky Metals began a large reverse circulation drilling program at the Tallebung tin project in New South Wales, according to a Tuesday filing with the Australian bourse.

The program comprises at least 70 drill holes for a total of 8,000 meters, targeting further shallow extensions to the tin mineralization, per the filing.

The first assay results from the program are expected in May.

Sky Metals' shares fell 7% in recent trade.

data.title: Wanhua Chemical Group 2024 Profit Down 22%; Shares Fall 4%
data.description:
Wanhua Chemical Group posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 13.0 billion yuan, down 22% from 16.8 billion yuan the previous year.

Earnings per share declined to 4.15 yuan from 5.36 yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The chemical manufacturer's revenue rose 3.8% year over year to 182.1 billion yuan from 175.4 billion yuan.

Wanhua Chemical Group declared a dividend of 0.73 per share.

Shares of the company were down 4% in recent trade.

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0324 GMT - Anhui Heli stands to benefit from recovering demand in China, Daiwa Capital Markets analysts say in a research report. Improving domestic demand is being overlooked by investors, say the analysts, noting data from China Construction Machinery Association showing the forklift operating rate rose to 53.3% in March from 47.6% in February and 46.9% in January. Also, the Chinese forklift manufacturer's 2024 profit from the U.S. was only 3% of total earnings, implying its financial impact from U.S. tariff increases should be limited, the analysts add. The brokerage raises the stock's rating to outperform from hold and the target price to CNY17.50 from CNY16.00. Shares are 0.9% lower at CNY16.09. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0321 GMT - UBS trims another 10% off its 2026 price forecast for lithium-rich spodumene, now projecting an average of US$763 a metric ton for next year versus spot price of US$770. It also pares its long-term price forecast by 7% to US$1,300. The bank's Australian analysts say that while the beleaguered lithium market is probably past its nadir, the battery ingredient is headed for a gradual recovery. The analysts pare their stock-price target on lithium producer Pilbara Minerals to A$1.50 from A$2.00. They upgrade Pilbara Minerals to a neutral from sell but cite valuation grounds. Pilbara Minerals is down 1.8% at A$1.39. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0316 GMT - Australian vehicle advertiser CAR Group keeps its bull at Jefferies despite its exposure to a potentially challenging U.S. consumer environment. Analyst Roger Samuel highlights CAR's resilience amid macro uncertainty, telling clients in a note that the business is the most diversified it has ever been. The U.S. recreational vehicle industry backdrop is challenging, but Samuel thinks it is highly unlikely that dealers there would turn off subscriptions to CAR's Trader Interactive platform. The platform's growth in media, trucks and boats should also help. Jefferies cuts the stock's target price by 9.2% to A$39.70 on a higher risk premium, but keeps a buy rating. Shares are down 1.5% at A$32.22. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0234 GMT - The ongoing U.S. tariff uncertainties could weigh on Malaysia's economy, particularly through supply chain disruptions and weakened external demand, Public IB analyst Eltricia Foong says in a note. While most of Malaysia's exports to the U.S., comprising largely electronics and machinery, are exempt for now, uncertainty over the duration of these exemptions may dampen investor confidence, she reckons. Foong lowers Malaysia's 2025 GDP forecast to 4.2% from 4.9%, citing trade risks and market volatility. Public IB maintains its KLCI end-2025 target at 1630, favoring stocks like CIMB Group, Telekom Malaysia, Tenaga Nasional and Gamuda. The KLCI is 0.1% higher at 1482.97. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0226 GMT - SCG Packaging faces increased risks in 2H stemming from the U.S.-China trade conflict, which is expected to affect demand for packaging paper in China, says UOB Kay Hian's Benjaphol Suthwanish in a research report. Although the packaging solutions provider is targeting a utilization rate of 90% or higher in 2025, the effects from U.S. tariffs will likely prevent the Thai company from achieving this target, the analyst says. Hence, there's potential downside risk to SCG Packaging's performance projections for 2025-2026, says the brokerage, which cuts its forecasts for 2025-2026 net profit by 9%. It lowers the stock's target price to THB13.20 from THB18.00 with an unchanged hold rating. Shares last closed at THB12.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0156 GMT - Malaysian bank stocks may remain rangebound in the near term amid uncertainty over U.S. tariffs, CIMB Securities analyst Rachel Huang says in a note. She says the banking industry may offer targeted loan relief programs, similar to those during the pandemic, to support exporters and suppliers hit by the tariffs. The programs during the pandemic prevented a sharp rise in loan impairment, she adds. Huang doesn't expect bank valuations to fall to pandemic lows, as the current situation isn't as severe. CIMB Securities maintains a neutral rating on Malaysian banks, maintaining buy calls on Alliance Bank Malaysia, Public Bank and RHB Bank mainly due to attractive dividend yields.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0152 GMT - Chinese President Xi Jinping's state visit to Malaysia could strengthen bilateral trade ties, especially as both nations may look to diversify away from the U.S., MIDF Research says in a note. The visit comes at a critical time amid rising trade tariffs and global economic tensions, the research house says. Xi's visit may boost Belt and Road initiatives in Malaysia and enhance connectivity and regional integration, it reckons. Malaysia's strong semiconductor and electronics sectors may continue to attract Chinese investment, driven by ongoing supply-chain diversification, it adds. MIDF notes that sectors likely to benefit include construction, technology and property, while tourism-related companies such as Capital A may also gain from stronger bilateral relations. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0147 GMT - Chinese shares decline, with the Shanghai Composite Index dropping 0.2% to 3254.51. The Shenzhen Composite Index is 0.2% lower and the ChiNext Price Index edges 0.1% lower. Investors are expecting Beijing to roll out more stimulus after it hit back against Trump's reciprocal tariffs. Among major stocks, semiconductor companies decline. SMIC drops 1.3% and Luxshare Precision Industry is 3.5% lower. Banks advance, with Bank of China adding 0.7% and Shanghai Pudong Development Bank 1.2% higher. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0140 GMT - Hong Kong shares are higher in early trade, supported by auto and tech stocks. Investors are tracking the latest developments in the U.S.-China trade tensions and any further macro stimulus from Beijing. Alibaba Group is leading the gains, up 3.0% and Kuaishou Technology is 1.5% higher. JD.com and Tencent are up 1.8% and 1.5%, respectively. BYD is up 1.3%. Meanwhile, home appliances and semiconductor stocks are lower with Haier Smart Home down 0.7% and Semiconductor Manufacturing International off 1.5%. The benchmark Hang Seng Index is up 0.3% at 21489.68 and the Hang Seng Tech Index is 0.3% higher.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0109 GMT - Malaysia's plantation sector may face headwinds ahead, says AmInvestment Bank analyst Gan Huey Ling in a note. While the three-month pause in U.S. tariffs gives customers time to stock up, the oleochemical industry could face challenges once the reprieve ends, she says. These include weaker global demand, rising competition from Indonesia and China, oversupply and margin pressure, she reckons. Still, palm oil demand could find support from China's reduced reliance on U.S. soybeans and a potential increase in the U.S. biodiesel mandate, she adds. However, Gan thinks selling prices for oleochemicals may remain under pressure in 2H due to lingering economic uncertainties. AmInvestment downgrades Malaysia's plantation sector to neutral from overweight, but maintains buy ratings on Genting Plantations, Johor Plantations, and TSH Resources. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0107 GMT - Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index rises 0.6% to 3571.44, tracking Wall Street gains. Investors took some relief from the latest U.S. tariff exemptions on consumer electronics, even though separate tariffs for the sector still loom, the Commerzbank Research team writes in a note. REITs lead the gains on the bourse, with CapitaLand Integrated Commercial Trust adding 2.0%, Frasers Centrepoint Trust rising 1.9% and Mapletree Industrial Trust advancing 1.8%. Among decliners, Jardine Matheson falls 2.2%, Keppel loses 0.7% and SATS is 0.4% lower. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0107 GMT - Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index is 0.2% higher at 1483.65. Chinese President Xi's visit is expected to lift the KLCI on optimism over potential Chinese investments, Malacca Securities says in a note. Malaysia, one of the largest rare earths processor outside China, could use the suspension of Chinese shipments of rare earths to the U.S. an opportunity to seek relief during the 90-day reprieve on Trump's tariffs, the brokerage says. Positive outcomes are also expected for the local semiconductor and glove sectors, it adds. Among gainers, YTL Power International rises 1.0% and Sunway Bhd. is 0.9% higher. Meanwhile, Kuala Lumpur Kepong is down 1.6% and Nestle (Malaysia) is 0.8% lower. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Hotel Properties Managing Director Steps Down Amid Scandal
data.description:
Hotel Properties' managing director, Ong Beng Seng will step down from the role effective April 29, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Monday.

The resignation comes after the company founder became involved in a scandal after he was accused of giving high-value gifts to ex-transport minister, S. Iswaran, who was given a prison sentence last year, according to a Reuters report.

data.title: UBS Cuts China's 2025 GDP Growth Forecast to 3.4% on U.S. TariffsMarket Talk
data.description: UBS downgrades China's 2025 economic growth forecast to 3.4%, assuming current tariff hikes remain and China rolls out additional stimulus. Earlier this month, the investment bank still held its China GDP growth forecast at 4%. Beijing set a 2025 growth target at around 5%. UBS anticipates China's exports to the U.S. to fall by two thirds in coming quarters and overall exports to fall 10% in the dollar term this year. The bank also expects the tariff shock could weigh China's economic growth to just 3% in 2026.(singaporeeditors@dowjones.com)

data.title: Argent BioPharma to Collaborate With AusCann on Cannabinoid-Based Pharmaceutical Development
data.description:
Argent BioPharma and AusCann Group Holdings signed a strategic heads of agreement to advance cannabinoid-based pharmaceutical development through the exchange of proprietary datasets and regulatory expertise, according to a Tuesday Australian bourse filing.

AusCann will license Argent BioPharma's CannEpil CMC and Dossier specifically for non-epilepsy-related pharmaceutical programs.

The companies will also gain access to AusCann's proprietary self-emulsifying hard-shell capsule formulation, Neuvis, including access to pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic data.

The firms will also establish a joint steering committee to coordinate research and development efforts as well as optimize regulatory strategies.

The agreement also includes a deferred licensing fee structure, with royalties on pharmaceutical products developed using jointly leveraged data, research and development, or assets.

Financial terms were not disclosed.

Argent BioPharma's shares were down almost 3% in recent Tuesday trade.

data.title: Market Chatter: Kingsford's One Marina Gardens Sells 37% of 937 Units For Sale
data.description:
Kingsford Group's One Marina Gardens sold around 37% of 937 units of property for sale during its launch weekend, Singapore Business Review reported Monday, citing data from PropNex Realty.

A majority of the sold units were one and two-bedroom units, the report said.

Foreign buyers are likely to buy more properties in Singapore with sales volume estimated to be between 7,500 to 8,500, the report said, citing Huttons Asia CEO Mark Yip.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: China's CATL hit near 2-week high on Q1 growth; broker lifts target price
data.description:
** Shares of EV battery maker CATL climb 2.9% to 230.45 yuan, on course for fourth straight session of gains

** CATL's first-quarter net profit grew 32.9% y/y, its fastest pace in nearly two years, while a 6.2% rise in revenue ended five quarters of decline

** Nomura maintains "buy" rating and raises TP to 331 yuan from 315 yuan after CATL's solid earnings expansion despite muted top-line growth

** The blue-chip CSI 300 Index eases 0.3% and the Shanghai Composite Index slips 0.2%

** CATL shares down 14.3% YTD

data.title: MHI Awarded a Study on CO2 Capture Modules for FPSOs
data.description:
TOKYO, Apr 15, 2025 - (JCN Newswire) - Mitsubishi Heavy Industries, Ltd. ("MHI") announced the signature of a study with Single Buoy Moorings Inc., the subsidiary of SBM Offshore ("SBM"), for the application of CO2 capture modules on future Floating Production Storage and Offloading vessels(Note1) ("FPSOs") for Petroleo Brasileiro S.A. (Petrobras). This study will be conducted toward typical implementation of CO2 capture solutions for FPSOs.

The module design for FPSOs is based on a combination of MHI's proprietary "Advanced KM CDR Process" CO2 capture technology(Note2) and SBM's Fast4Ward principles(Note3). The study will focus on capture of the CO2 emitted by onboard gas turbines, with analysis and evaluation to be carried out toward future commercialization.

The study is the first achievement of a partnership agreement between MHI and SBM in September 2023(Note4). The agreement, a response to rapid expansion in demand for decarbonization, aims to accelerate business development in CO2 capture solution for FPSOs, to contribute to achievement of carbon neutrality on a global scale.

MHI Group has formally declared its commitment to achieving carbon neutrality by 2040 (MISSION NET ZERO), and the Company is now working strategically to decarbonize both the energy demand and supply sides. A core element of the Company's "Energy Transition," which targets decarbonization on the energy supply side, is the development of a carbon capture, utilization and storage (CCUS) value chain integrating diverse sources of carbon emissions with modes for carbon storage and utilization. Going forward, MHI Group will continue to proactively promote its CCUS business worldwide, applying its proprietary CO2 capture technologies, contributing as a solutions provider to reducing greenhouse gas emissions on a global scale, and developing further solutions that contribute to environmental protection.

1FPSOs are floating systems for vessels that produce, store and offload oil and gas at sea.2The "Advanced KM CDR Process" was jointly developed with The Kansai Electric Power Co., Inc. 3Fast4Ward is a program targeting reduced cycle time - from oil/gas excavation to supply - using standardized FPSO hulls developed by SBM Offshore, a leader in the FPSO industry.4For further information on MHI's collaboration with SBM Offshore, refer to the following press release:https://www.mhi.com/news/230915.html

About MHI Group's CO2 capture technologiesMHI Group has been developing the "KM CDR Process" (Kansai Mitsubishi Carbon Dioxide Recovery Process) and the "Advanced KM CDR Process" in collaboration with the Kansai Electric Power Co., Inc. since 1990. As of April 2025, the Company has delivered 18 plants adopting these processes. The "Advanced KM CDR Process" adopts the "KS-21" solvent, which incorporates technological improvements over the amine-based "KS-1" and offers superior regeneration efficiency and lower deterioration than the "KS-1", and it has been verified to provide excellent energy saving performance, reduce operation costs, and result in low amine emissions.

For further information on MHI Group's CO2 capture plants: www.mhi.com/products/engineering/co2plants.html

About MHI Group

Mitsubishi Heavy Industries (MHI) Group is one of the world's leading industrial groups, spanning energy, smart infrastructure, industrial machinery, aerospace and defense. MHI Group combines cutting-edge technology with deep experience to deliver innovative, integrated solutions that help to realize a carbon neutral world, improve the quality of life and ensure a safer world. For more information, please visit www.mhi.com or follow our insights and stories on spectra.mhi.com.

Source: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries, Ltd.

Copyright 2025 JCN Newswire . All rights reserved.

data.title: Market Chatter: Korea Customs Service Launches Crackdown on Antidumping Duty Evasion
data.description:
The Korea Customs Service began a special inspection into circumvention dumping to catch firms avoiding antidumping tariffs through a minor product alteration, Yonhap News reported Monday.

Running through July 22, the probe targets potential diversion of low-cost goods-especially from countries hit by US tariffs-into the South Korean market. Offenders could face back taxes and criminal charges, the report said.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Australia's DroneShield rises as Bell Potter hikes PT on industry tailwinds
data.description:
** Shares of DroneShield rise 9.2% to A$1.13, their highest levels since March 21

** The drone technology co announced a package of five standalone, repeat contracts from a military customer for A$32.2 million ($20.43 million)

** Bell Potter says that global military expenditure is forecast to increase, providing macro tailwinds to defence sector and underpinning drone technology as key area for investment

** Brokerage increases revenue forecasts by 21%, 13% and 13% for CY25, CY26 and CY27, respectively

** Hikes price target to A$1.30 from A$1.10, and retains "buy" rating

** Stock up 47.7%, YTD

($1 = 1.5758 Australian dollars)
